questions.question: Which is a RUN-ON sentence?
choices: When I found my dog, I was happy.
To make my dad proud, I cleaned my room.
My head hurt and I was tired, but I studied anyway.
I helped my teacher because I couldn't go to recess, I had a cold.
questions.explanation: Two complete sentences joined only with a comma.

questions.question: Which word does NOT belong?
questions.explanation: First three are TREES. Lily is a FLOWER.

questions.question: Paragraph 3 is about what?
questions.explanation: Paragraph 3 says soil from volcanoes is rich.
questions.passageText:
Volcanoes are mountains with a special feature: they can erupt. Deep underground, melted rock called magma builds up. When pressure gets too high, it bursts through the surface.

Volcanic eruptions can be small or huge. Some volcanoes leak lava slowly. Others explode with great force, sending ash, rocks, and gas miles into the air. The biggest eruptions can change weather around the world for years.

Despite their danger, volcanoes also do good. The cooled lava breaks down into rich soil that plants love. Many volcanic islands are some of the most fertile places on Earth.

questions.question:
What comes next?
5, 11, 17, 23, ___
What is the main idea of paragraph 2?
choices: Earth has a solid surface and water
Jupiter has more gravity than Earth
Jupiter is more interesting to study
Earth and Jupiter have very different physical properties
questions.explanation: Paragraph 2 lists differences between Earth and Jupiter.
questions.passageText:
Our solar system contains eight planets, each with unique characteristics. The four inner planets — Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars — are called terrestrial planets because they have solid, rocky surfaces. The four outer planets — Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune — are called gas giants because they are made mostly of gases.

Earth and Jupiter could hardly be more different. Earth has a solid surface where people can walk, liquid water in its oceans, and an atmosphere that supports life. Jupiter, on the other hand, has no solid surface at all. It is a swirling mass of gases with storms larger than our entire planet.

Despite these differences, both planets share some similarities. Both have magnetic fields that protect them from harmful solar radiation. Both have moons. And both orbit the same star.

questions.question: Best topic for paragraph about benefits of journal writing:
choices: Journal writing helps you process emotions, remember experiences, and improve writing.

questions.explanation: Without control plants (no talking), she can't tell if talking is the cause. The plants might grow anyway.

wrongExplanations.1: Volume doesn't matter for the experiment.

questions.question: Most people who exercise feel happy. Pat exercises every day. Pat is most likely:
questions.explanation: MOST = probably. Pat probably feels happy.

choices: Planets are different but share some traits

wrongExplanations.2: Wrong — only outer planets are gas giants.

questions.passageText:
Our solar system contains eight planets, each with unique characteristics. The four inner planets — Mercury, Venus, Earth, and Mars — are called terrestrial planets because they have solid, rocky surfaces. The four outer planets — Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune — are called gas giants because they are made mostly of gases.

Earth and Jupiter could hardly be more different. Earth has a solid surface where people can walk, liquid water in its oceans, and an atmosphere that supports life. Jupiter, on the other hand, has no solid surface at all. It is a swirling mass of gases with storms larger than our entire planet.

Despite these differences, both planets share some similarities. Both have magnetic fields that protect them from harmful solar radiation. Both have moons. And both orbit the same star.

questions.question: The recipe required only ______ ingredients — flour, water, and salt.

Order math homework steps:
1. Check your answers
2. Read the problem carefully
3. Solve the problem
4. Underline what's being asked
Emma notices the lights flicker every time the dryer starts. What's the BEST test that the dryer causes flickering?
choices: Run the dryer 10 times — see if lights flicker each time
Don't run the dryer — see if lights ever flicker
Try both — run dryer sometimes, don't other times — compare
questions.explanation: Compare both conditions to see if dryer is the cause.

wrongExplanations.0: Doesn't show what happens without the dryer.

questions.question: All squares are quadrilaterals. All quadrilaterals have four sides. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Squares → quadrilaterals → 4 sides. Squares have 4 sides.

questions.question: What can you infer about the threat to the migration?
choices: Monarchs will go extinct in 10 years
Both their winter homes and breeding plants are at risk
questions.explanation: Logging + herbicides = both winter and breeding stages threatened.

wrongExplanations.3: OPPOSITE — passage says milkweed is the ONLY plant.

questions.passageText: Monarch butterflies travel up to 3,000 miles from summer homes in the U.S. and Canada to winter roosts in central Mexico. No single butterfly completes the round trip — the monarchs flying south are great-great-grandchildren of those that flew north. Yet they find the exact same trees their ancestors used. Scientists discovered monarchs navigate using the sun's position and Earth's magnetic field. They have an internal compass in their antennae. The migration is now under threat: logging in Mexico has destroyed roosts; herbicides have killed milkweed — the only plant where monarchs lay eggs.

questions.question: Paragraph 2 mainly explains what?
choices: How brain speed lets you react
questions.passageText:
Your brain is faster than you might think. When you see something — a ball flying toward you, for example — your brain processes the image in less than 13 thousandths of a second. That's faster than a camera flash.

This speed lets you react. By the time you 'realize' you've seen the ball, your hand may already be moving to catch it. The brain handles the work before you're even conscious of it.

Different parts of the brain handle different tasks. The visual cortex sees. The motor cortex moves muscles. The two work together with very little delay. That's why athletes who practice can make split-second moves seem effortless.

questions.question: Ages: 30,35,40. Average?
Rule: "+2 each time." Start at 7. 10th term?

Column B: 50
Dr. Lin notices her morning coffee correlates with her good mood. What's the BEST experiment?
choices: Drink coffee every morning for a month
Drink coffee some mornings, decaf others — compare moods
Ask others if coffee helps mood
questions.explanation: Comparison with vs. without caffeine = real test.

wrongExplanations.3: More isn't a test of cause.

questions.question: What does "feeling something heavy in her chest" probably mean?
questions.explanation: Sara feels sadness about her grandmother changing.
questions.passageText: Every Saturday for as long as Sara could remember, she had visited her grandmother's garden. They would weed, water, and pick vegetables together. Grandma always seemed to know exactly what each plant needed — when it needed water, when it had a bug problem, when it was ready to harvest. "You'll learn," Grandma would say with a smile. This Saturday, Grandma sat in a chair instead of kneeling in the dirt. "My back doesn't like the work anymore," she said. "Sara, would you do the watering today?" Sara nodded and picked up the watering can, feeling something heavy in her chest.

wrongExplanations.2: PARTIAL — but the bigger feeling is unhappiness.

questions.passageText: Aunt Joan stood at the door with two big suitcases and a wide smile. "I'm here for the weekend!" she announced. Mom managed a smile, but Sara could see her clenching her jaw. Their guest room was full of boxes from moving. The kitchen was a mess. Mom hadn't slept well in days. Aunt Joan walked past Mom and into the kitchen. "Oh, you've been busy!" she chirped. "Don't worry, I'll fix everything up while I'm here."

What is paragraph 1 mostly about?
choices: How much marine life lives in reefs
questions.explanation: Paragraph 1 says 25% of marine species live there.
questions.passageText:
Coral reefs are sometimes called 'rainforests of the sea' because they support an incredible variety of ocean life. Though reefs cover less than 1% of the ocean floor, they are home to about 25% of all marine species.

Reefs are built by tiny animals called coral polyps. Over thousands of years, polyps build hard skeletons that grow into massive structures. Fish, turtles, sharks, and many other creatures live in and around these structures.

Today, coral reefs face serious problems. Warmer ocean temperatures cause coral 'bleaching,' which can kill the coral. Pollution and overfishing add to the threats. Many scientists worry that reefs may not survive without major changes.

questions.question: What can you infer was happening in Tomas's body when his fingers felt cold?
choices: His body was reacting to nervousness
questions.passageText: Tomas had been practicing for the piano recital for six months. The day arrived. He sat backstage, listening to the others play. They were all good — really good. By the time it was his turn, his fingers felt cold. He walked out, sat down, and looked at the keys. For a moment, he forgot the first note. Panic rose in his chest. Then he closed his eyes and let his hands move. They knew what to do. The music came out smooth and full. Five minutes later, he stood and bowed to applause that seemed to last forever.

questions.passageText: Monarch butterflies travel up to 3,000 miles from summer homes in the U.S. and Canada to winter roosts in central Mexico. No single butterfly completes the round trip — the monarchs flying south are great-great-grandchildren of those that flew north. Yet they find the exact same trees their ancestors used. Scientists discovered monarchs navigate using the sun's position and Earth's magnetic field. They have an internal compass in their antennae. The migration is now under threat: logging in Mexico has destroyed roosts; herbicides have killed milkweed — the only plant where monarchs lay eggs.

What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Earthquakes — what they are, what they do, and how we prepare
questions.explanation: Covers cause, effect, and preparation — all three paragraphs.
questions.passageText:
Earthquakes happen when huge sections of the Earth's crust suddenly slip past each other. The crust is made of giant pieces called tectonic plates, which are always moving slowly. Sometimes they get stuck. When they finally slip, the energy released causes the ground to shake.

Most earthquakes are too small to feel. But powerful earthquakes can damage buildings, break roads, and even cause tsunamis. Scientists called seismologists study earthquakes to understand them better.

While we cannot prevent earthquakes, we can prepare. Buildings in earthquake zones are designed to sway without falling. Schools practice 'drop, cover, and hold on' drills. These efforts save lives.

questions.question: Each of the workers ______ a uniform.
How was Olivia feeling at the start?
questions.explanation: Sitting alone, watching others, third day, doesn't know anyone.
questions.passageText: Olivia sat at lunch alone. It was her third day at the new school, and she still didn't know anyone. She watched as kids laughed in groups around her. She poked at her food. Then a girl from her math class — Tara — walked over with her tray. "Mind if I sit?" Tara asked. Olivia shook her head and felt a small smile creep onto her face. Tara sat down and said, "I noticed you got that hard problem in math today. I couldn't figure it out." "It was tricky," Olivia said. "Want me to show you how I did it?"

questions.question: All squares are rectangles. All rectangles have four sides. Therefore:
questions.explanation: Squares are rectangles. Rectangles have 4 sides. Squares have 4 sides.

wrongExplanations.2: All squares have 4 sides per the chain.

questions.explanation: DOCTOR helps PATIENT. TEACHER helps STUDENT. Person who helps to person being helped.

wrongExplanations.2: Store and customer is location/person — different.
wrongExplanations.3: Mother and father are family relationship.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?
choices: Sleep is important for body and brain in many ways
questions.passageText:
Every animal sleeps. Even fish. Why? For a long time, scientists weren't sure. But research has revealed several reasons.

First, sleep helps the body repair itself. Cells fix damage, growth hormones do their work, and the immune system gets stronger. Without enough sleep, you're more likely to get sick.

Second, sleep helps the brain. While you sleep, your brain sorts through what you learned that day. Memories get stored. New connections form. Without sleep, you can't focus, can't learn well, and can't remember.

Finally, sleep gives the brain time to clean itself. During sleep, fluid washes through the brain, removing waste from the day. Without this cleanup, brain damage builds up over time.

The robot's tools are smaller than human hands, allowing surgeons to make tinier cuts. This means patients heal faster. The robot also cancels out the natural shaking of human hands, making the procedure safer.
